name: z-skills
description: Zack's personal collection of custom skills for Claude Code.

plugins.name: z-learn
plugins.description: Turns coding sessions into guided learning — builds, refactors, and debugs incrementally while narrating the reasoning, calibrated to what you already know.

plugins.name: z-build
plugins.description: A streamlined explore→plan→implement→test→review loop with a live visual companion for ideation. Does what gsd/superpowers do for a coding task, but leans on Opus's native planning and in-context execution instead of heavy subagent pipelines and on-disk scaffolding. Keeps the disciplines an LLM skips; drops the ceremony.

plugins.name: z-map
plugins.description: Maintains a compact, in-repo, incrementally refreshable codebase map so a cold session gets oriented in seconds and navigates to the right file:line on demand. A SessionStart hook injects the map's orientation and flags staleness; the skill generates and incrementally refreshes the map.

plugins.name: z-log
plugins.description: A lean, in-repo episodic memory: a curated log of decisions, gotchas, and open threads so the next session recalls the why, not just the what. Model-authored on demand (no per-tool capture); a SessionStart hook injects the bounded digest and nudges when work has happened since the last entry.

plugins.name: z-commit
plugins.description: Turns a finished working tree into clean, atomic, well-described commits you review and run yourself — proposes groupings and messages, flags what shouldn't ship, and never stages or commits for you.

plugins.name: z-ground
plugins.description: Ground-truth API verification: pin the installed version, read the actual surface (types, source, --help), follow the repo's existing call sites, and probe with a throwaway script when still unsure. Never code from memory against a version-sensitive API — memory of an API is a hypothesis, not a fact.

plugins.name: z-subagent
plugins.description: Delegation discipline for subagents: decide when to delegate vs. read it yourself, then dispatch with a real contract — one goal, a scope fence, an exact return shape, a hard size cap, and file:line grounding with confidence tags — so agents return compact, verifiable results instead of dumps.

plugins.name: z-upgrade
plugins.description: A disciplined dependency, framework, runtime, or toolchain upgrade: read the migration notes, inventory usage, upgrade one layer at a time, verify runtime behavior (not just compilation), and record deferred deprecations. Never blindly bumps versions.
